title: fagara rhoifolia var. niederleinii
intro: Fagara rhoifolia var. niederleinii, also known as Chilean pepper tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to South America, belonging to the Rutaceae family. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds like limonene and α-pinene, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. This plant is used to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties, and also has potential benefits for digestive issues, including indigestion and diarrhea. Fagara rhoifolia var. niederleinii is commonly used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its therapeutic properties and provide relief from various health issues.
